title: Sessions
summary: Explains how Hermes stores, names, resumes, searches, and prunes sessions across CLI and messaging platforms, including cross-platform handoff and auto-lineage on compression.

cli_commands.purpose: Resume the most recent CLI session
Resume a named session (latest in lineage)
Resume a specific session by ID
Prune sessions older than retention threshold

config_keys.description: How to display previous conversation on resume: full | minimal

Days to retain ended sessions before pruning
VACUUM state.db after pruning to reclaim disk space

slash_commands.purpose: Transfer session to a messaging platform

code_examples.caption: Session management commands
code_examples.code:
hermes sessions list
hermes sessions export backup.jsonl
hermes sessions rename <id> "debugging auth flow"

code_examples.caption: Auto-prune configuration
code_examples.code:
sessions:
  auto_prune: true
  retention_days: 90
  vacuum_after_prune: true
  min_interval_hours: 24

code_examples.caption: Group session isolation
code_examples.code: group_sessions_per_user: true  # per-user isolation in groups (default)

gotchas: auto_prune ships disabled — enable explicitly if state.db is affecting performance
Pruning only deletes ended sessions — active sessions are never auto-pruned
hermes sessions prune only deletes ended sessions; use delete for a specific session
Sessions with active background processes are never auto-reset by reset policies
Session IDs: CLI/TUI use 6-char hex suffix, gateway uses 8-char suffix
After VACUUM, state.db shrinks; without it, SQLite doesn't reclaim space on DELETE
v0.12 added session-delete from the /resume picker UI — previously had to delete from disk manually
v0.12 added opt-in auto-resume of last session on TUI launch
full_text:
# Sessions

# Sessions

Hermes Agent automatically saves every conversation as a session. Sessions enable conversation resume, cross-session search, and full conversation history management.

## How Sessions Work

Every conversation — CLI, Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Teams, etc. — is stored as a session with full message history in two systems:

1. **SQLite database** (`~/.hermes/state.db`) — structured session metadata with FTS5 full-text search
2. **JSONL transcripts** (`~/.hermes/sessions/`) — raw conversation transcripts including tool calls

The SQLite database stores:
- Session ID, source platform, user ID
- Session title (unique, human-readable)
- Model name and configuration
- Full message history (role, content, tool calls, tool results)
- Token counts (input/output)
- Timestamps (started_at, ended_at)
- Parent session ID (for compression-triggered session splitting)

## Session Sources

| Source | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `cli` | Interactive CLI |
| `telegram` | Telegram messenger |
| `discord` | Discord server/DM |
| `slack` | Slack workspace |
| `whatsapp` | WhatsApp messenger |
| `signal` | Signal messenger |
| `matrix` | Matrix rooms |
| `mattermost` | Mattermost channels |
| `email` | Email (IMAP/SMTP) |
| `sms` | SMS via Twilio |
| `cron` | Scheduled cron jobs |
| `batch` | Batch processing runs |

## CLI Session Resume

```bash
# Resume the most recent CLI session
hermes --continue
hermes -c

# Resume a named session (picks most recent in lineage)
hermes -c "my project"

# Resume by session ID
hermes --resume 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4
hermes -r 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4

# Resume by title
hermes --resume "refactoring auth"
```

### Conversation Recap on Resume

Shows a compact recap panel with recent turns before the live prompt.

- Shows **user messages** (gold `●`) and **assistant responses** (green `◆`)
- Truncates long messages; collapses tool calls to a count
- Caps at the last 10 exchanges

```yaml
display:
  resume_display: minimal   # default: full
```

## Cross-Platform Handoff

```bash
# Inside a CLI session
/handoff telegram
```

Transfers the live conversation to a messaging platform's home channel. The gateway re-binds the session and the reply lands in a new thread.

## Session Naming

### Auto-Generated Titles

Hermes automatically generates a short descriptive title (3–7 words) after the first exchange using a fast auxiliary model.

### Setting a Title Manually

```
/title my research project
```

Or from shell:
```bash
hermes sessions rename 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4 "refactoring auth module"
```

**Title rules:**
- Unique — no two sessions share the same title
- Max 100 characters
- Normal Unicode is fine (emoji, CJK, accented chars)

### Auto-Lineage on Compression

```
"my project" → "my project #2" → "my project #3"
```

When resuming by name, it automatically picks the most recent in the lineage.

## Session Management Commands

```bash
# List recent sessions
hermes sessions list
hermes sessions list --source telegram
hermes sessions list --limit 50

# Export
hermes sessions export backup.jsonl
hermes sessions export telegram-history.jsonl --source telegram
hermes sessions export session.jsonl --session-id 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4

# Delete
hermes sessions delete 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4
hermes sessions delete 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4 --yes

# Rename
hermes sessions rename 20250305_091523_a1b2c3d4 "debugging auth flow"

# Prune old sessions
hermes sessions prune
hermes sessions prune --older-than 30
hermes sessions prune --source telegram --older-than 60 --yes

# Stats
hermes sessions stats
```

## Session Search Tool

The agent has a built-in `session_search` tool using SQLite's FTS5 engine.

**FTS5 query syntax:**
- Simple keywords: `docker deployment`
- Phrases: `"exact phrase"`
- Boolean: `docker OR kubernetes`, `python NOT java`
- Prefix: `deploy*`

## Per-Platform Session Tracking

| Chat Type | Default Key Format |
|-----------|--------------------|
| DM | `agent:main:<platform>:dm:<chat_id>` |
| Group chat | `agent:main:<platform>:group:<chat_id>:<user_id>` |
| Group thread | `agent:main:<platform>:group:<chat_id>:<thread_id>` |

### Shared vs Isolated Group Sessions

```yaml
group_sessions_per_user: true   # default: per-user isolation
group_sessions_per_user: false  # shared session per room
```

### Session Reset Policies

- **idle** — reset after N minutes of inactivity
- **daily** — reset at a specific hour each day
- **both** — reset on whichever comes first
- **none** — never auto-reset

Sessions with active background processes are never auto-reset.

## Storage

| What | Path |
|------|------|
| SQLite database | `~/.hermes/state.db` |
| Gateway transcripts | `~/.hermes/sessions/` |
| Gateway index | `~/.hermes/sessions/sessions.json` |

## Session Expiry and Cleanup

```yaml
sessions:
  auto_prune: true         # opt in — default is false
  retention_days: 90
  vacuum_after_prune: true
  min_interval_hours: 24
```

Auto-prune only deletes ended sessions; active sessions are never auto-pruned.
